expectations.text: Picks 'closer to business policy' as correct
expectations.evidence: 'The closer to business policy camp is right.'

expectations.text: Level = distance from I/O, not genericness
expectations.evidence: 'level is defined by distance from I/O'

expectations.text: Concrete examples: business class as high-level vs reused utility as low-level
expectations.evidence: Detailed expense-reimbursement example: ReimbursementPolicy (high-level) vs MoneyMath/SlackNotifier (low-level despite reuse)

expectations.text: Protection direction: keep DB/UI/framework churn from climbing up
expectations.evidence: 'when the database vendor, the JSON library, or the HTTP framework changes, the rules of the business must not have to change'

expectations.text: Cautions against conflating reuse-count with levelness
expectations.evidence: 'Stable ≠ high-level. StringUtils is stable and widely reused; it is the lowest level imaginable.'
